subject: Computer Networking
title: 5G and the Next Generation
description: A quick, practical guide to 5G and the Next Generation.

hook.text: 5G promises gigabit speeds on your phone. But it's not just about faster downloads — it's about new possibilities.

buildup.text: 5G uses higher frequencies and denser cell towers. More bandwidth per user, lower latency than 4G.

discovery.text: Network slicing creates virtual networks per use case: low-latency for surgery, high-bandwidth for video.

twist.text: 5G mmWave travels short distances. Full coverage needs many more towers than 4G — costly and slow to deploy.

climax.text: Self-driving cars, remote surgery, and smart cities all depend on 5G's speed and reliability to function safely.

punchline.text: 5G isn't just faster 4G. It's infrastructure for a new era.

quiz.question: What is network slicing in 5G?
options: Creating virtual networks with different performance characteristics
Physically cutting cables to reroute traffic
Dividing the radio spectrum equally among all users
